text: Three statements are given, followed by three conclusions numbered I, II and III. Assuming the statements to be true, even if they seem to be at variance with commonly known facts, decide which of the conclusions logically follow(s) from the statements. Statements: All spoons are forks. All forks are knives. No knife is a spatula. Conclusions: I. Some knives are spoons. II. No fork is a spatula. III. Some spoons are spatulas.

options.text: Only conclusions I and II follow

solution:
**Step 1:** Draw quick set/Venn relations from each statement.
**Step 2:** Test each conclusion independently using only definite relations.
**Step 3:** Keep only conclusions that must be true.
**Answer:** Option C (Only conclusions I and II follow).
subject: REASONING

text: Read the given statements and conclusions carefully.
